1.texto_original: Ora, a fome era gravíssima na terra.
1.normal:
Devotional Study of Gênesis 43:1

"Ora, a fome era gravíssima na terra."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Gênesis 43:1

Philological Analysis

"Ora, a fome era gravíssima na terra."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Ora, a fome era gravíssima na terra."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Responderam eles: O teu servo, nosso pai, está bem; ele ainda vive. E abaixaram a cabeça, e inclinaram-se."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

29.texto_original: Levantando os olhos, José viu a Benjamim, seu irmão, filho de sua mãe, e perguntou: É este o vosso irmão mais novo de quem me falastes? E disse: Deus seja benévolo para contigo, meu filho.
29.normal:
Devotional Study of Gênesis 43:29

"Levantando os olhos, José viu a Benjamim, seu irmão, filho de sua mãe, e perguntou: É este o vosso irmão mais novo de quem me falastes? E disse: Deus seja benévolo para contigo, meu filho."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
29.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Gênesis 43:29

Philological Analysis

"Levantando os olhos, José viu a Benjamim, seu irmão, filho de sua mãe, e perguntou: É este o vosso irmão mais novo de quem me falastes? E disse: Deus seja benévolo para contigo, meu filho."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

32.texto_original: Serviram-lhe, pois, a ele à parte, e a eles também à parte, e à parte aos egípcios que comiam com ele; porque os egípcios não podiam comer com os hebreus, porquanto é isso abominação aos egípcios.

Devotional Study of Gênesis 43:32

"Serviram-lhe, pois, a ele à parte, e a eles também à parte, e à parte aos egípcios que comiam com ele; porque os egípcios não podiam comer com os hebreus, porquanto é isso abominação aos egípcios."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

34.texto_original: Então ele lhes apresentou as porções que estavam diante dele; mas a porção de Benjamim era cinco vezes maior do que a de qualquer deles. E eles beberam, e se regalaram com ele.
34.normal:
Devotional Study of Gênesis 43:34

"Então ele lhes apresentou as porções que estavam diante dele; mas a porção de Benjamim era cinco vezes maior do que a de qualquer deles. E eles beberam, e se regalaram com ele."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.
